conclusion: Per-row full-attention input RMSNorm alone is not a c8 fullnative fix. Continue targeting grouped full-attention output/composition or rowchunk semantics rather than promoting larger chunks or only replaying the later input norm.

interpretation.negative_result: Forcing only full-attention input RMSNorm to per-row does not make the c8 fullnative L8/decode1 trace green. It keeps the layer4 attn_input bit drift and increases full-attention failed stages from the baseline 12 to 18; token equality also fails by layer_limit 6 in this diagnostic.
interpretation.no_code_change: The retained-bench/runtime rowchunk2 auto policy was already present, so no policy patch was made in this iteration.
interpretation.root_target: The layer4 drift is not solved by replaying the later full-attention input RMSNorm. It remains consistent with upstream full-attention output numerical drift feeding layer4 linear-attention state/RMSNorm, or broader grouped full-attention composition/ordering, while rowchunk2 remains exact.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: focused c8 full-attention input/RMSNorm diagnostic directly tests the layer4 amplification hypothesis with artifact evidence. It is negative, but it materially narrows the fix target and makes no performance claim.
